name: Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular from Mirandela
name_original: Subida ao Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular desde Mirandela
description: Challenging 30.7-mile road cycling route to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção from Mirandela, with rewarding views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Mirandela is characterized by its diverse landscapes, shaped by the Tua River and its surrounding valleys. The region features varied terrain, from relatively flat sections along riverbanks to challenging ascents into wooded slopes and the Bornes Mountain Range. Cyclists can expect routes that navigate through river valleys, offering scenic views and opportunities for elevation gain. The area's natural features provide a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Mirandela The most popular road cycling route is Vale de Asnes e Casa do Povo do Romeu — circular desde Mirandela, a 42.7 miles (68.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular from Mirandela, a difficult 30.7 miles (49.5 km) path. This route features challenging climbs towards a notable sanctuary, providing panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to the Miradouro de Santa and the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular from Mirandela, a 53.7 miles (86.4 km) trail leading through scenic lookouts and demanding ascents, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Mirandela is defined by the Tua River valleys, wooded slopes, and the Bornes Mountain Range. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding ascents. The routes in Mirandela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mirandela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mirandela?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Mirandela, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes shaped by the Tua River and its surrounding valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Mirandela?
A: Road cycling in Mirandela offers a diverse terrain. You'll find relatively flat sections along the banks of the Tua, Tuela, and Rabaçal rivers, as well as more challenging ascents into wooded slopes and the Bornes Mountain Range. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403241477/vale-de-asnes-e-casa-do-povo-do-romeu-circular-desde-mirandela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale de Asnes e Casa do Povo do Romeu — circular desde Mirandela&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mirandela?
A: While many routes in Mirandela are rated moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. For those looking for a less strenuous ride, focusing on the flatter sections along the riverbanks can provide a good starting point. The region's river valleys offer more relaxed sections before venturing into more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Mirandela?
A: Yes, Mirandela offers numerous challenging routes for experienced cyclists. Over 10 routes are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402872517/ascent-to-the-sanctuary-of-nossa-senhora-da-assuncao-circular-from-mirandela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular from Mirandela&lt;/a&gt;, which involves challenging climbs, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402894809/ascent-to-the-miradouro-de-santa-and-the-sanctuary-of-nossa-senhora-da-assuncao-circular-from-mirandela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Miradouro de Santa and the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção — circular from Mirandela&lt;/a&gt;, a longer trail with demanding ascents and scenic lookouts.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Mirandela?
A: The Mirandela region is rich in natural beauty. You'll cycle alongside the picturesque Tua River, which forms a beautiful 'water mirror' next to the historic Old Bridge. The routes also traverse the valleys of the Tuela and Rabaçal rivers. Southwest of Mirandela, the Regional Natural Park of Vale do Tua offers dramatic canyons, diverse foliage like boxwood and cork oaks, and a rich birdlife. Wooded slopes and the Bornes Mountain Range provide stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Mirandela's routes often pass by significant landmarks. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6343504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; (also known as the Medieval Bridge or Old Bridge) over the Tua River is a notable point of interest. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6346607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Community House of Romeu&lt;/a&gt; and other small architectural treasures in charming villages like Abreiro, Frechas, and Avantos, offering glimpses into local culture.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Mirandela?
A: The road cycling routes in Mirandela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunities to discover local culture and historical landmarks.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Mirandela?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Mirandela are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1403241477/vale-de-asnes-e-casa-do-povo-do-romeu-circular-desde-mirandela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale de Asnes e Casa do Povo do Romeu — circular desde Mirandela&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402872396/from-mirandela-to-the-olive-fields-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mirandela to the olive fields — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in Mirandela?
A: Mirandela, as a town, offers various parking options, particularly near the town center and along the Tua River. Cyclists can typically find suitable parking spaces to begin their routes. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability.

Q: Are there opportunities to stop for refreshments or explore villages along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Cycling through the Mirandela region provides ample opportunities to discover small architectural treasures and historic centers in nearby villages such as Abreiro, Frechas, and Avantos. These villages often have local cafes or establishments where you can stop for refreshments and experience local culture.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mirandela?
A: The Trás-os-Montes region, where Mirandela is located, generally experiences hot, dry summers and cold winters. Therefore, the best times for road cycling are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October), when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. These seasons offer comfortable conditions for both challenging ascents and leisurely rides along the river.

Q: Are there any repurposed railway lines for cycling in the Mirandela area?
A: Yes, the former railway line connecting Mirandela to Bragança, known as the E-Mob Main Route (formerly Tua Line), has been repurposed for cycling. This offers a unique and often flatter way to traverse parts of the region's landscapes, though it's particularly popular for electric mountain bikes.
tips: Mirandela, known as the "Princess of the Tua," is a charming town located on the banks of the Tua River in the Trás-os-Montes region. It stands out for its historical charm, beautifully maintained riverside gardens, and an authentic atmosphere that blends heritage, nature, and genuine flavors. The town offers a well-balanced experience: it's not overcrowded, yet it has enough attractions for a half-day to two-day getaway.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route typically takes around 2 hours and 45 minutes to complete, though individual times may vary based on fitness and pace.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'hard road ride', requiring very good fitness due to its significant climbs. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção route start?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts and ends in Mirandela. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride. However, the final ascent to the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Assumption is along a beautiful stone road.

faqs.question: What are the key features of the climb to the Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Assunção?
faqs.answer: The specific climb to the Santuário de Nossa Senhora da Assunção is 5.8 kilometers long, ascending 389 meters from an elevation of 58 meters to 447 meters at the peak. It has an average gradient of 6.7% and a maximum gradient of 12%, making it a significant challenge.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Assumption itself, which offers spectacular panoramic views over the Valle do Tua. The route also begins and ends in the charming town of Mirandela.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly passes through sections of other official trails, including the Trilho do Tua, Calçada da Igreja, Largo da Igreja, Praça do Município, Calçada Manuel Maria Barbosa do Bocage, Rua do Arco, Travessa da Cadeia Velha, Travessa Clemente Menéres, Rua das Arçãs, Rua do Canelho, and Calçada de São Cosme.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a challenging racebike route predominantly on paved roads, it is generally not suitable for dogs. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this ride?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to visit the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Assumption.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during the spring or autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions, making the challenging ascent more difficult.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the Sanctuary or along the route?
faqs.answer: While the Sanctuary itself is a hermitage atop a hill, the circular route starts and ends in Mirandela, where you will find various cafes and restaurants to refuel before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Assumption
